pubmed_links: [Magno et al. (2019) Alzheimer's disease phospholipase C-gamma-2 (PLCG2) protective variant is a functional hypermorph](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30711010/); [Moutinho et al. (2023) Genetic variants of phospholipase C-gamma-2 alter the phenotype and function of microglia and confer differential risk for Alzheimer's disease](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37714148/)
summary: PLCG2, expressed in brain microglia, harbors variants with opposing effects on Alzheimer's disease risk and modulates microglial TREM2 signaling and neuroinflammation; while its primary neurological associations are with neurodegeneration rather than classic neuropsychiatric disorders, its role in microglial immune signaling is relevant to broader neuropathology. (emerging evidence)
